title: University of Queensland — AI Policy Staged Crawl Report
summary: University of Queensland (QS 2026 rank ~42) has a structured approach to AI in assessment. UQ uses a two-option system in course profiles: Option 1 prohibits AI in in-person/secure assessment; Option 2 permits AI use with mandatory referencing. AI outputs used without attribution constitute academic misconduct. Turnitin AI detection was disabled mid-2025 as unreliable. Microsoft Copilot Chat is described by the UQ Library as UQ's enterprise AI tool with a higher level of data security and privacy than other AI tools. Students must acknowledge where they used AI in assessment. Five official sources were verified and fetched; eight sources were rejected as faculty-specific, news, research, or event content.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This is a staged crawl; claims have not undergone human review.
Some UQ policy documents (e.g., Student Code of Conduct, Academic Integrity Framework PDFs) were not separately fetched in this run.
Faculty-specific guidance (e.g., Law School) was excluded from university-level claims.
sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
